vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permissions on a backup gshadow file to remove all access. This is a standard permission hardening step and does not affect system boot, networking, or authentication mechanisms that could lock out users.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potentially removes legitimate read/write access for scripts that might expect the backup file
